vocabulary.context: Weasel and water shrew
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 黄鼠狼和水鼩

vocabulary.definition: n. 小鹿；v.i. 摇尾乞怜, 奉承
vocabulary.context: fished, also culled deer fawns and to their kill scraps
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 捕鱼，还宰杀小鹿和它们的残骸

vocabulary.definition: n. 鼬鼠, 狡猾的人, 告密者, 狡辩之辞；v.i. 躲避, 告密
vocabulary.context: Weasel and water shrew
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 黄鼠狼和水鼩

vocabulary.definition: n. 蔓延, 风行一时, 超过限度；v.t. 蔓延, 蹂躏, 超过, 泛滥；v.i. 泛滥, 超载限度；[计] 超限
vocabulary.context: compelled to meander, is less prone to overrun.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 被迫蜿蜒，不易发生超限

vocabulary.definition: n. 秃鹫, 贪婪的人；[法] 贪婪而残酷者, 劫掠成性者
vocabulary.context: came vulture and coyote, long gone in the region
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 秃鹫和郊狼来了，它们早已在该地区消失了

vocabulary.definition: n. 世纪, 百年
vocabulary.context: of the mid century.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 上世纪中叶

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 将(音素、单词等)置于上下文中研究, ( 通过介绍来龙去脉) 使( 事件、 活动等) 溶入背景
vocabulary.context: Belgium's African diaspora has been arguing for years that the racist exhibits need to be removed or better contextualized.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 比利时的非洲侨民多年来一直主张种族主义展览需要被移除或更好地结合实际情况

vocabulary.definition: adj. 开拓性的，独创的
vocabulary.context: That was true of Belgium until 2005, Gryseels says, when a ground-breaking exhibition at the museum directly addressing colonial abuses sparked nation-wide debate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Gryseels 说，直到 2005 年，比利时的情况都是如此，当时博物馆举办的一场开创性的展览直接讨论了殖民时期的虐待行为，引发了全国性的辩论

vocabulary.definition: adj. 新古典主义的
vocabulary.context: Such was the success of the 1897 exhibition that the King made it permanent, using funds from his interests in the Congo to construct the grand neoclassical building which houses the collection today.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1897 年的展览取得了巨大成功，国王将其永久化，并利用他在刚果的利益所得的资金建造了宏伟的新古典主义建筑，如今该建筑内藏有藏品

vocabulary.definition: adj. 人种志的
vocabulary.context: Some of these will be consigned to the store rooms, others will re-emerge along with items from the world's largest collection of Central African ethnographic objects in zones called "Man & Society" and "Landscape and Biodiversity."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中一些将被存放在储藏室中，另一些将与世界上最大的中非民族志物品收藏一起重新出现在"人与社会"和"景观与生物多样性"区域

vocabulary.definition: adj. 与原物一样大小的
vocabulary.context: It would take a determined visitor to the Royal Museum for Central Africa to locate the life-sized likeness of one of history's most brutal colonial rulers carved from ivory.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 只有坚定的游客来到中非皇家博物馆才能找到用象牙雕刻的历史上最残暴的殖民统治者之一的真人大小的肖像

vocabulary.definition: adj. 左派的, 左翼的
vocabulary.context: The only apology for any of Belgium's actions in the Congo was in 2002, when the foreign minister offered "profound and sincere regrets" for the assassination in 1961 of Patrice Lumumba, the first post-independence leader, a left-wing populist, who was shot dead with the complicity of Belgian officers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 比利时对在刚果的任何行为唯一一次道歉是在2002年，当时外交部长对1961年独立后第一位领导人、左翼民粹主义者帕特里斯·卢蒙巴(Patrice Lumumba)被暗杀表示"深切而真诚的遗憾"，卢蒙巴在比利时军官的共谋下被枪杀

vocabulary.definition: adj. 玫瑰色的；希望的；乐观的
vocabulary.context: It is not just the museum's rose-tinted vision of colonial history that will be brought up-to-date.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 博物馆将更新的不仅仅是对殖民历史的玫瑰色愿景

vocabulary.definition: adj. 乡愁的, 怀旧的；[医] 怀乡病的
vocabulary.context: Daughton says that former colonial powers are reluctant to offer blanket apologies because that could open them up to claims for financial compensation, while they also risk a backlash at home where many voters remain nostalgic for the days of empire.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 道顿说，前殖民国家不愿意提供全面的道歉，因为这可能会让他们面临经济赔偿的要求，同时他们也面临着在国内遭到强烈反对的风险，因为许多选民仍然怀念帝国时代

vocabulary.definition: n. 圆形建筑, 圆形大厅
vocabulary.context: Visitors will no longer enter the museum in the rotunda where the gold statues stand.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 游客将不再从金雕像矗立的圆形大厅进入博物馆

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 交付, 储蓄, 委托, 寄存；[经] 托卖, 寄存, 寄销
vocabulary.context: Some of these will be consigned to the store rooms, others will re-emerge along with items from the world's largest collection of Central African ethnographic objects in zones called "Man & Society" and "Landscape and Biodiversity."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中一些将被存放在储藏室中，另一些将与世界上最大的中非民族志物品收藏一起重新出现在"人与社会"和"景观与生物多样性"区域

vocabulary.definition: adv. 体贴地, 体谅地
vocabulary.context: Though this is a story written for boys, it may be read by some business men, who will allow me to suggest that a refusal kindly and considerately expressed loses half its bitterness, and often inspires hope, instead of discouragement.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然这是一个为男孩写的故事，但它可能会被一些商人读到，他们会让我建议，善意而体贴地表达的拒绝会减少一半的痛苦，并且常常会激发希望，而不是沮丧

vocabulary.definition: n. 笔记本, 钱袋, 皮夹, 经济来源
vocabulary.context: "He opened my trunk and took out my pocketbook while I was absent on business."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "当我出差时，他打开了我的行李箱并拿出了我的钱包。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 绅士的, 绅士派头的
vocabulary.context: The gentleman was favorably impressed by Frank's polite and gentlemanly manners.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 弗兰克的礼貌和绅士风度给这位绅士留下了深刻的印象

vocabulary.definition: adv. 招待周到地, 善于款待地, 亲切地
vocabulary.context: "If ever you come to Craneville, I should like to have you stay a few days with me on my farm," said Mr. Peters, hospitably. "We are plain folks, but will treat you about right."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "如果您来克兰维尔，我希望您能在我的农场里住几天，"彼得斯先生热情地说。 "我们都是普通人，但会善待你。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 办理, 处理；v.i. 作交易, 谈判
vocabulary.context: I told him that he could transact his business here."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我告诉他可以在这里处理生意。"

vocabulary.definition: adv. 懊悔地, 抱歉地
vocabulary.context: Just now, in his extremity, the boy thought regretfully of the fortune he had lost.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 刚才，在极度的痛苦中，男孩遗憾地想起自己失去的财产

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不习惯的, 不惯的, 异乎惯例的, 不寻常的
vocabulary.context: It pleased Frank to find that, in spite of his inexperience, he was able to be of service to one more unaccustomed than himself to city scenes and city ways.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 弗兰克很高兴地发现，尽管他缺乏经验，但他能够为比他自己更不熟悉城市风光和城市道路的人提供服务

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 支付, 支出；[经] 支付或准备支付
vocabulary.context: Had he been so situated as to be earning enough to defray all his expenses, he would scarcely have given a thought to it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果他的收入足以支付他所有的开支，他几乎不会考虑这个问题

vocabulary.definition: n. 乡绅, 大地主, 治安官；v.t. 随侍, 护卫；v.i. 当乡绅
vocabulary.context: "The squire-Squire Jackson, of our village-perhaps you may have heard of him?"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们村里的乡绅--杰克逊乡绅--也许你听说过他？"

vocabulary.definition: n. 所有者；[经] 所有人, 资本主, 业主
vocabulary.context: "You advertise for a boy," he said to a tall, stout man, who chanced to be the proprietor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "你为一个男孩做广告，"他对一个高大粗壮的男人说，他碰巧是店主

vocabulary.definition: adj. 身材苗条的
vocabulary.context: "Give me a dozen healthy infants, well-formed, and my own specified world to bring them up in and I'll guarantee to take any one at random and train him to become any type of specialist I might select - doctor, lawyer, artist, merchant-chief and,...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "给我一打健康的婴儿，发育良好，在我自己指定的世界里抚养他们，我保证随机挑选任何一个，训练他成为我可能选择的任何类型的专家--医生、律师、艺术家、商人酋长，......

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不易动感情的, 缺乏感情的, 冷漠的, 铁石心肠的
vocabulary.context: Albert, described as "on the whole stolid and unemotional," showed no fear of any of these stimuli.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阿尔伯特被描述为"总体上冷漠无情"，他对任何这些刺激都没有表现出恐惧

vocabulary.definition: n. 行为主义者；[医] 行为主义者
vocabulary.context: Behaviorism as a movement in psychology appeared in 1913 when John Broadus Watson published the classic article "Psychology as the behaviorist views it." John Watson proposed that the process of classical conditioning (based on Pavlov's observations) was able to explain all aspects of human psychology.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 行为主义作为心理学运动出现于 1913 年，当时约翰·布罗德斯·沃森（John Broadus Watson）发表了经典文章"行为主义者看待心理学"。约翰·沃森提出经典条件反射过程（基于巴甫洛夫的观察）能够解释人类心理的各个方面

vocabulary.definition: n. 恐怖病, 恐惧症, 惧怕, 憎恶；[医] 恐怖[症]
vocabulary.context: Watson and Rayner had shown that classical conditioning could be used to create a phobia.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 沃森和雷纳表明，经典条件反射可以用来制造恐惧症

vocabulary.definition: n. 职业, 天命, 天职, 才能, 行业；[法] 行业, 职业, 职业上的才能
vocabulary.context: ...lawyer, artist, merchant-chief and, yes, even beggar-man and thief, regardless of his talents, penchants, tendencies, abilities, vocations and the race of his ancestors" (Watson, 1924, p.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...律师、艺术家、商人首领，是的，甚至是乞丐和小偷，无论他的天赋、嗜好、倾向、能力、职业和祖先的种族如何"（Watson，1924，第 17 页）

vocabulary.definition: n. 刺激, 激励, 刺激品；[医] 刺激特, 刺激
vocabulary.context: Everything from speech to emotional responses were simply patterns of stimulus and response.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从言语到情绪反应的一切都只是刺激和反应的模式

vocabulary.definition: adj. 缺乏热情的, 神经麻木的
vocabulary.context: Albert, described as "on the whole stolid and unemotional," showed no fear of any of these stimuli.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阿尔伯特被描述为"总体上冷漠无情"，他对任何这些刺激都没有表现出恐惧

vocabulary.definition: adv. 伦理上
vocabulary.context: In a famous (though ethically dubious) experiment, Watson and Rayner (1920) showed that it did.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在一个著名的（尽管伦理上可疑的）实验中，沃森和雷纳（1920）证明了这一点

vocabulary.definition: adj. 古典的, 正统派的, 经典的；[医] 古典的; 标准的, 典型的
vocabulary.context: Behaviorism as a movement in psychology appeared in 1913 when John Broadus Watson published the classic article "Psychology as the behaviorist views it." John Watson proposed that the process of classical conditioning (based on Pavlov's observations) was able to explain all aspects of human psychology.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 行为主义作为心理学运动出现于 1913 年，当时约翰·布罗德斯·沃森（John Broadus Watson）发表了经典文章"行为主义者看待心理学"。约翰·沃森提出经典条件反射过程（基于巴甫洛夫的观察）能够解释人类心理的各个方面

vocabulary.definition: n. 国家地位；[法] 成为国家的事实或状态, 民族主义
vocabulary.context: The country has had only three generational leaders in 70 years of nationhood: Kim Il-sung (referred to as the "Eternal President"), his son Kim Jong-il, and his grandson, Kim Jong-un.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该国建国70年来只有三代领导人：金日成（被称为"永远的总统"）、他的儿子金正日和他的孙子金正恩

vocabulary.definition: n. 中毒；[医] 中毒
vocabulary.context: Furthermore, if North Korea is welcomed into an international community, potential allies must contend with the serious human rights abuses taking place under Kim Jong-un's leadership: a regime which reportedly holds over 60,000 citizens in brutal work camps and uses public executions, assassinations, and poisonings to create a pandemic of fear.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，如果朝鲜受到国际社会的欢迎，潜在的盟友就必须应对金正恩领导下发生的严重侵犯人权行为：据报道，该政权将 60,000 多名公民关押在残酷的工作营中，并利用公开处决、暗杀和投毒手段制造恐惧大流行

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 开封, 开启, 使解除束缚
vocabulary.context: Whether Kim Jong-un and his government are willing to unseal the Hermit Kingdom and work towards a better life for the people remains to be seen.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 金正恩和他的政府是否愿意开启隐士王国并为人民过上更好的生活而努力还有待观察

vocabulary.definition: adj. 生殖的, 世代的, 代与代之间的, 一代的, 生育的
vocabulary.context: The country has had only three generational leaders in 70 years of nationhood: Kim Il-sung (referred to as the "Eternal President"), his son Kim Jong-il, and his grandson, Kim Jong-un.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该国建国70年来只有三代领导人：金日成（被称为"永远的总统"）、他的儿子金正日和他的孙子金正恩

vocabulary.definition: n. 翻领
vocabulary.context: The Kim dynasty has created a cult of personality in North Korea: citizens are required to wear lapel pins with portraits of a member of the Kim family, bow in front of statues of the three men, and are required to lay flowers and gifts at paintings of the leaders.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 金氏王朝在朝鲜制造了一种个人崇拜：公民必须佩戴印有金氏家族成员肖像的胸针，在三人的雕像前鞠躬，并被要求在领导人的画前敬献鲜花和礼物

vocabulary.definition: adv. 错误地, 被误解地
vocabulary.context: For North Korean citizens, willingly or mistakenly breaking the many rules of their government can result in grave consequences.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于朝鲜公民来说，自愿或错误地违反政府的许多规则可能会导致严重后果

vocabulary.definition: adv. 根据传说, 根据传闻, 据报道
vocabulary.context: Furthermore, if North Korea is welcomed into an international community, potential allies must contend with the serious human rights abuses taking place under Kim Jong-un's leadership: a regime which reportedly holds over 60,000 citizens in brutal work camps and uses public executions, assassinations, and poisonings to create a pandemic of fear.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，如果朝鲜受到国际社会的欢迎，潜在的盟友就必须应对金正恩领导下发生的严重侵犯人权行为：据报道，该政权将 60,000 多名公民关押在残酷的工作营中，并利用公开处决、暗杀和投毒手段制造恐惧大流行

vocabulary.definition: n. 忠贞, 效忠
vocabulary.context: Where an average day may include mandatory "meetings" pledging allegiance to an all-powerful ruler.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 平均一天可能包括强制性"会议"，宣誓效忠全能的统治者

vocabulary.definition: n. 千的五次方, 百万之四次方；adj. 千之五次方的
vocabulary.context: EARTH-In a seemingly unstoppable cycle of carnage that has become tragically commonplace throughout the biosphere, sources confirmed this morning that natural selection has killed an estimated 38 quadrillion organisms in its bloodiest day yet.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 地球--在一场似乎不可阻挡的大屠杀循环中，悲剧性地在整个生物圈中变得司空见惯，消息来源今天早上证实，自然选择在迄今为止最血腥的一天杀死了估计 38 万亿个生物体

vocabulary.definition: n. 对流层；[医] 对流层
vocabulary.context: ...observers, natural selection left a trail of dead in such conflict-plagued regions as sub-Saharan Africa, the Pacific Ocean, and the troposphere, with the fatalities including slain emperor penguins, coral snakes, and blue-green algae, as well as several swarms of blue-winged wasps, an assortment of hyacinth clusters, 131 orangutans, and various...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...观察者，自然选择在撒哈拉以南非洲、太平洋和对流层等饱受冲突困扰的地区留下了死亡痕迹，死亡事件包括被杀死的帝企鹅、珊瑚蛇和蓝绿藻，以及几群蓝翅黄蜂、各种风信子簇、131只猩猩和各种......

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无准备的；无配备的；无能力的
vocabulary.context: All were reportedly unequipped to escape the terror sweeping their ecosystems.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 据报道，所有人都没有装备来逃离席卷其生态系统的恐怖

vocabulary.definition: n. (生物分类上的)门, 语系；[医] 门[生物分类]
vocabulary.context: "Even as we speak, this vicious, unfeeling butcher is sizing up new victims, with no regard to their age, gender, or phylum.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "就在我们说话的时候，这个恶毒、无情的屠夫正在打量新的受害者，不考虑他们的年龄、性别或门类

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 摄取, 咽下, 吸取
vocabulary.context: At press time, sources confirmed the latest onslaught in the natural selection process had claimed the lives of a cottonwood tree, 800 million saltwater amoebae, and a Columbus, OH delivery driver who accidentally ingested a food item containing peanuts.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 截至发稿时，消息人士证实，自然选择过程中的最新猛烈攻击夺去了一棵三叶杨树、8亿只咸水变形虫的生命，以及俄亥俄州哥伦布市一名送货司机意外摄入含有花生的食品的生命

vocabulary.definition: adv. 有系统地, 有方法地
vocabulary.context: "We have just witnessed the deadliest moment in a genocide that aims to eradicate entire swaths of given populations, taking them out methodically and with complete indifference.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们刚刚目睹了一场种族灭绝中最致命的时刻，这场种族灭绝旨在消灭整个特定人群，有条不紊地、完全冷漠地消灭他们

vocabulary.definition: adj. 肠的, 肠内的；[医] 肠的
vocabulary.context: "What we're seeing here is the work of a hardened, practiced killer," said Yale University evolutionary biologist Richard Prum, describing the brutal process through which a massive number of victims-among them thousands of starfish, countless patches of moss, and entire colonies of intestinal protozoa-were massacred with little to no warning.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 耶鲁大学进化生物学家理查德·普鲁姆（Richard Prum）描述了残酷的过程："我们在这里看到的是一个冷酷无情、经验丰富的杀手的杰作"，他描述了大批受害者--其中包括数千只海星、无数苔藓和整个肠道原生动物群落--在几乎没有任何警告的情况下被屠杀的残酷过程

vocabulary.definition: n. 乱闹, 乱冲, 暴跳；v.i. 乱闹, 狂暴
vocabulary.context: In what many are calling its most grotesque tactic, the killer appeared to single out the most vulnerable organisms-particularly the young and the physically weak-for its murderous rampage, slaughtering them without mercy as other members of their species fled in panic.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在许多人称之为最怪诞的策略中，杀手似乎挑出了最脆弱的生物--尤其是年轻人和体弱多病的人--进行凶残的狂暴，毫不留情地屠杀它们，而其他物种的成员则惊慌失措地逃跑

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不矫揉造作的, 自然的, 未受影响的, 未被感动的；[经] 不受影响的
vocabulary.context: Virtually no species was unaffected by yesterday's killing spree, experts stated.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 专家表示，几乎没有一个物种没有受到昨天的杀戮狂潮的影响

vocabulary.definition: n. 大屠杀, 残杀
vocabulary.context: EARTH-In a seemingly unstoppable cycle of carnage that has become tragically commonplace throughout the biosphere, sources confirmed this morning that natural selection has killed an estimated 38 quadrillion organisms in its bloodiest day yet.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 地球--在一场似乎不可阻挡的大屠杀循环中，悲剧性地在整个生物圈中变得司空见惯，消息来源今天早上证实，自然选择在迄今为止最血腥的一天杀死了估计 38 万亿个生物体

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无法令人满足的
vocabulary.context: It's fun!" incredibly unsatisfying.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这很有趣！"非常不满意

vocabulary.definition: adj. 适合家庭的; 有利于家庭生活的
vocabulary.context: This adults-only extreme attraction went beyond the typical startling lights and sounds and animated characters found in a family-friendly haunted house.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个仅限成人的极限景点超越了家庭友好型鬼屋中常见的令人震惊的灯光、声音和动画人物

vocabulary.definition: n. 反应, 反动, 反应性, 活动性, 反应率；[医] 反应性
vocabulary.context: Analysis of the EEG data revealed widespread decreases in brain reactivity from before to after among those whose mood improved.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 脑电图数据分析显示，情绪改善的人从之前到之后大脑反应性普遍下降

vocabulary.definition: n. 猛砍者, 乱砍者, 好殴斗者, 恃强欺弱的人, 刀, 泥刀, 剃刀片, 剑, 浆纱机
vocabulary.context: Few horror movies have achieved similar notoriety, and it's credited with kicking off the steady stream of slasher flicks that followed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 很少有恐怖电影能获得类似的恶名，它被认为开启了随后源源不断的恐怖片的序幕

vocabulary.definition: n. 自知之明
vocabulary.context: Choosing to do fun, scary activities may also serve as a way to practice being scared, building greater self-knowledge and resilience, similar to rough-and-tumble play.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 选择进行有趣、可怕的活动也可以作为练习害怕的一种方式，建立更多的自我认识和适应能力，类似于打闹游戏

vocabulary.definition: n. 脑(电)波, 灵感
vocabulary.context: We also used mobile EEG technology to compare 100 participants' brainwave activity as they sat through 15 minutes of various cognitive and emotional tasks before and after the attraction.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们还使用移动脑电图技术来比较 100 名参与者在景点前后进行 15 分钟的各种认知和情感任务时的脑电波活动

vocabulary.definition: n. 留心, 警觉
vocabulary.context: Studies of those who practice mindfulness meditation have made a similar observation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对练习正念冥想的人的研究也得出了类似的观察结果

vocabulary.definition: adj. 以死亡为主题的, 可怕的
vocabulary.context: Why, then, would anyone want to spend their time and money to watch such macabre scenes filled with depressing reminders of just how unfair and scary our world can be?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那么，为什么有人愿意花时间和金钱观看这些令人沮丧的场景，这些场景充满了令人沮丧的提醒，让我们知道我们的世界是多么不公平和可怕？

vocabulary.definition: n. （某些非洲和加勒比地区的宗教及恐怖故事中）靠巫术起死回生的僵尸( zombie的复数形式 ); 无生气的人, 麻木迟钝的人
vocabulary.context: As I experienced myself after all kinds of scary adventures in Japan, Colombia, and all over the U.S., confronting a horde of zombies can actually make you feel pretty invincible.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如我在日本、哥伦比亚和美国各地经历过各种可怕的冒险之后所经历的那样，面对一大群僵尸实际上会让你感觉自己所向无敌

vocabulary.definition: n. 讨厌, 嫌恶
vocabulary.context: Regardless of your taste (or distaste) for all things horror or thrill-related, an adventurous and curious mindset can benefit everyone.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 无论您对所有与恐怖或刺激相关的事物的品味（或厌恶）如何，冒险和好奇的心态都可以使每个人受益

vocabulary.definition: n. 非正式
vocabulary.context: This is simply the most recent chapter of a centurylong debate over the place of informality and immodesty in our dress: how short can that skirt be?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这只是长达一个世纪的争论的最新章节，争论的焦点是我们的着装中是否存在不拘礼节和不端庄的地方：那条裙子能多短？

vocabulary.definition: n. 体育专栏作家
vocabulary.context: In 1936, a sportswriter named Paul Gallico argued that female athletes and their clothing were offensive.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1936 年，一位名叫保罗·加利科 (Paul Gallico) 的体育作家认为，女运动员及其服装具有攻击性

vocabulary.definition: adj. 分为两部分；v. （指道路、河流、树枝等）分岔, 分成两支( bifurcate的过去式和过去分词 ); 使分枝, 使分叉
vocabulary.context: Starting in the late 1920s, shorts became the much-contested replacement for bloomers, the puffy-legged, bifurcated garment worn under long skirts.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从 20 年代末开始，短裤成为备受争议的灯笼裤替代品，灯笼裤是一种穿在长裙下面的浮肿腿部分叉服装

vocabulary.definition: n. （尤指政治动乱的）爆发点; 政治动乱地区；危险地区
vocabulary.context: Sportswear, which can be both informal and immodest, has served as a flashpoint in these debates - particularly for women.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 运动装既可以是非正式的，也可以是不端庄的，它已成为这些辩论中的焦点--特别是对于女性而言

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不谦虚的, 冒失的
vocabulary.context: Sportswear, which can be both informal and immodest, has served as a flashpoint in these debates - particularly for women.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 运动装既可以是非正式的，也可以是不端庄的，它已成为这些辩论中的焦点--特别是对于女性而言

vocabulary.definition: n. 女式紧身连衣裤

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了他的动作和一张脸就像

vocabulary.definition: adj. 行为的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一个新的行为问题迅速成为一个关键挑战--如何处理另一种干扰

vocabulary.definition: n. 中小学的校长
vocabulary.context: Matt Hancock, who is in charge of digital policy, said: "I admire headteachers who do not allow mobiles to be used during the school day.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 负责数字政策的马特·汉考克 (Matt Hancock) 表示："我钦佩那些不允许在上课期间使用手机的校长

vocabulary.definition: adj. 依赖的, 信赖的
vocabulary.context: Some teachers use interactive learning activities such as the game-based platform Kahoot!, which is free but is reliant on students being able to access mobile devices.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些教师使用互动学习活动，例如基于游戏的平台 Kahoot!，该平台是免费的，但依赖于学生能够访问移动设备

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 组织, 有机化, 给予生机
vocabulary.context: Smartphones are used to make calls, run businesses and organise social lives.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 智能手机用于打电话、经营业务和组织社交生活

vocabulary.definition: n. 自我训练, 自我修养, 自律；[法] 自律, 自我约束, 自我修养
vocabulary.context: Exam results are important, but so too are wider skills such as using technology appropriately and safely, and having the self-discipline to regulate the use of mobile technology - knowing the right time and place to fire up a smart phone.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 考试成绩很重要，但更广泛的技能也很重要，例如适当、安全地使用技术，以及规范移动技术使用的自律 - 知道启动智能手机的正确时间和地点

vocabulary.definition: n. 计算者, 计算器；[计] 计算器
vocabulary.context: It contains a writing tool, a calculator, and a huge encyclopedia - and I can't imagine anyone calling for those to be banned at the school gates.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它包含一个书写工具、一个计算器和一本巨大的百科全书--我无法想象有人呼吁在学校门口禁止这些东西

vocabulary.definition: n. 获得, 获得物；[经] 收购, 招揽, 取得
vocabulary.context: Acquisition of knowledge is not enough - we must ensure young people are ready for the next stage of their lives.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 仅仅获得知识是不够的--我们必须确保年轻人为人生的下一阶段做好准备

vocabulary.definition: n. 行为, 举止；[计] 特性, 性能, 特点, 行为, 动作, 状态
vocabulary.context: The rationale, I suspect, is that by removing the catalyst for poor behaviour (the mobile phone) we remove the issue.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我怀疑其基本原理是，通过消除不良行为的催化剂（手机），我们可以解决这个问题

vocabulary.definition: n. 说服, 安抚, 抚慰, 调和；[经] 调解
vocabulary.context: "Let this incident, touching and beautiful as it is, impart to our Washington authorities a lesson in conciliation."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "让这起感人而美丽的事件给我们华盛顿当局上一堂和解的课。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 纪念, 纪念物, 庆典
vocabulary.context: The holiday was Memorial Day, an annual commemoration was born in the former Confederate States in 1866 and adopted by the United States in 1868.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个节日是阵亡将士纪念日，一年一度的纪念活动于1866年在前南部邦联诞生，并于1868年被美国采用

vocabulary.definition: n. 敌意, 仇视
vocabulary.context: "The action of the ladies on this occasion, in burying whatever animosities or ill-feeling may have been engendered in the late war towards those who fought against them, is worthy of all praise and commendation," wrote one paper.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一篇论文写道："女士们这次的行动，埋葬了战争后期对那些与她们作战的人可能产生的任何敌意或敌意，值得所有赞扬和赞扬。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 产生, 引起；v.i. 发生, 形成
vocabulary.context: "The action of the ladies on this occasion, in burying whatever animosities or ill-feeling may have been engendered in the late war towards those who fought against them, is worthy of all praise and commendation," wrote one paper.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一篇论文写道："女士们这次的行动，埋葬了战争后期对那些与她们作战的人可能产生的任何敌意或敌意，值得所有赞扬和赞扬。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 宽宏大量
vocabulary.context: Other reports of reciprocal magnanimity circulated in the North, including the gesture of a 10-year-old who made a wreath of flowers and sent it to the overseer of the holiday, Colonel Leaming, in Lafayette, Indiana, with the following note attached, published in The New Hampshire Patriot on July 15, 1868:
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其他关于相互慷慨的报道在北方流传，包括一名 10 岁孩子制作花环并将其寄给印第安纳州拉斐特的节日监督者利明上校的举动，并附有以下注释，发表在 1868 年 7 月 15 日的《新罕布什尔爱国者》上：

vocabulary.definition: n. 赞美, 称赞；v.t. 赞美, 称赞
vocabulary.context: On May 9, 1866, the Cleveland Daily Leader lauded the Southern women during their first Memorial Day.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1866 年 5 月 9 日，《克利夫兰日报领袖》在第一个阵亡将士纪念日期间赞扬了南方妇女

vocabulary.definition: adj. 宽宏大量的, 有雅量的；[法] 气量大的, 宽大的, 高尚的
vocabulary.context: The New York Commercial Advertiser, recognizing the magnanimous deeds of the women of Columbus, Georgia, echoed the sentiment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 《纽约商业广告报》认识到佐治亚州哥伦布市妇女的慷慨行为，也表达了同样的观点

vocabulary.definition: n. 征服者, 胜利者
vocabulary.context: "It struck me that the South was holding out a friendly hand, and that it was our duty, not only as conquerors, but as men and their fellow citizens of the nation, to grasp it."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "令我震惊的是，南方伸出了友好的手，我们有责任不仅作为征服者，而且作为国家的人和他们的同胞，抓住它。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 行星仪, 天文馆, 天象图
vocabulary.context: For example, one story is about a character's summer job at a planetarium.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，一个故事是关于一个角色在天文馆的暑期工作

vocabulary.definition: n. 古怪举动, 俏皮话, 急转
vocabulary.context: Quirks of psychology make us miss mistakes
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 心理学的怪癖让我们错过错误

vocabulary.definition: n. 假情报
vocabulary.context: Our psychological quirks put us at risk of falling for misinformation, disinformation, and propaganda.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们的心理怪癖使我们面临陷入错误信息、虚假信息和宣传的风险

vocabulary.definition: adv. 可靠地, 可信赖地
vocabulary.context: We reliably find positive effects of reading the correct information within the story - participants are more likely to answer "Who was the first person to step foot on the moon?" correctly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们可靠地发现阅读故事中正确信息的积极影响 - 参与者更有可能回答"谁是第一个踏上月球的人？"正确

vocabulary.definition: n. 错误的消息, 误传；[法] 错误的消息, 误传
vocabulary.context: We also see negative effects of reading the misinformation - participants are both less likely to recall that Jupiter is the largest planet and they are more likely to answer with Saturn.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们还看到了阅读错误信息的负面影响--参与者都不太可能记得木星是最大的行星，而他们更有可能回答土星

vocabulary.definition: n. 发现, 察觉, 探测；[计] 检波; 检测
vocabulary.context: For example, in the sentence-by-sentence detection task participants caught about 30 percent of the errors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，在逐句检测任务中，参与者发现了大约 30% 的错误

vocabulary.definition: n. 信任, 信赖, 受信赖的人(或事)；[法] 信任, 信赖, 信心
vocabulary.context: Many of the interventions my colleagues and I have implemented to try to reduce people's reliance on the misinformation have failed or even backfired.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我和我的同事为减少人们对错误信息的依赖而实施的许多干预措施都失败了，甚至适得其反

vocabulary.definition: n. 插入, 介入, 调停；[经] 干预
vocabulary.context: Many of the interventions my colleagues and I have implemented to try to reduce people's reliance on the misinformation have failed or even backfired.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我和我的同事为减少人们对错误信息的依赖而实施的许多干预措施都失败了，甚至适得其反

vocabulary.definition: adv. 方法学(methodological的变形)
vocabulary.context: "My main issue is that methodologically, there have been so many problems with Milgram's research that we have to start re-examining the textbook descriptions of the research," she said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我的主要问题是，在方法论上，米尔格拉姆的研究存在很多问题，我们必须开始重新审查教科书对这项研究的描述，"她说

vocabulary.definition: adj. 方法的
vocabulary.context: But many psychologists argue that even with methodological holes and moral lapses, the basic finding of Milgram's work, the rate of obedience, still holds up.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但许多心理学家认为，即使存在方法论漏洞和道德失误，米尔格拉姆研究的基本发现--服从率--仍然成立

vocabulary.definition: n. 情境决定行为论
vocabulary.context: Though the term didn't exist at the time, Milgram was a proponent of what today's social psychologists call situationism: the idea that people's behavior is determined largely by what's happening around them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然当时还不存在这个术语，但米尔格拉姆是当今社会心理学家所谓的情境主义的支持者：人们的行为很大程度上取决于他们周围发生的事情

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 记错
vocabulary.context: And in the past few years, a new wave of researchers have dedicated themselves to reshaping it, arguing that Milgram's lessons on human obedience are, in fact, misremembered - that his work doesn't prove what he claimed it does.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在过去的几年里，新一波的研究人员致力于重塑它，他们认为米尔格拉姆关于人类服从的教训实际上被错误地记住了--他的工作并没有证明他声称的那样

vocabulary.definition: adj. 预录的
vocabulary.context: Until they emerged from the lab, the participants didn't know that the shocks weren't real, that the cries of pain were pre-recorded, and that the learner - railroad auditor Jim McDonough - was in on the whole thing, sitting alive and unharmed in the next room.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 直到他们从实验室出来之前，参与者并不知道电击不是真实的，痛苦的哭声是预先录制的，而且学习者--铁路审计师吉姆·麦克唐纳--参与了整个事情，活着坐在隔壁房间里，没有受伤

vocabulary.definition: n. 合编者
vocabulary.context: Andrews and a co-editor of the Journal of Social Issues' special edition.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 安德鲁斯和《社会问题杂志》特别版的联合编辑

vocabulary.definition: adj. 被制作成电视的
vocabulary.context: ...puns out of the word "shocking"; a controversial French documentary disguised as a game show; episodes of Law and Order and Bones; a made-for-TV movie with William Shatner; a jewelry collection (bizarrely) from the company Enfants Perdus; and most recently, the biopic The Experimenter, starring Peter Sarsgaard as the title character - and this...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...双关语"令人震惊"；一部伪装成游戏节目的有争议的法国纪录片； 《法律与秩序》和《骨头》的剧集；威廉·夏特纳 (William Shatner) 制作的电视电影； Enfants Perdus 公司的珠宝系列（奇怪的是）；最近，由彼得·萨斯加德主演的传记片《实验者》--而这部......

vocabulary.definition: n. 批准, 确认；[计] 证实, 检验
vocabulary.context: Milgram, who was born in New York City in 1933 to Jewish immigrant parents, came to view his studies as a validation of Arendt's idea - but the Holocaust had been at the forefront of his mind for years before either of them published their work.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 米尔格拉姆 1933 年出生于纽约，父母是犹太移民，他逐渐将自己的研究视为对阿伦特思想的验证，但在他们发表自己的作品之前，大屠杀多年来一直是他脑海中最重要的问题

vocabulary.definition: n. 平凡, 陈腐
vocabulary.context: In 1963, the same year that Milgram published his studies, writer Hannah Arendt coined the phrase "the banality of evil" to describe Eichmann in her book on the trial, Eichmann in Jerusalem.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1963年，即米尔格拉姆发表研究成果的同一年，作家汉娜·阿伦特在她关于审判的书《艾希曼在耶路撒冷》中创造了"邪恶的平庸"一词来形容艾希曼

vocabulary.definition: adj. 档案的, 档案中的
vocabulary.context: "If you look at acts of human atrocity, they've hardly diminished over time," he said, and news of the abuse at Abu Ghraib was surfacing around the same time that Yale's archival material was digitized, a perfect storm of encouragement for scholars to turn their attention once again to the question of what causes evil.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "如果你看看人类的暴行，你会发现它们几乎没有随着时间的推移而减少，"他说，而关于阿布格莱布监狱虐待事件的消息大约在耶鲁大学档案材料数字化的同时浮出水面，这是一场完美的风暴，鼓励学者们再次将注意力转向邪恶的根源问题

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 巡回法院检察官叶夫根尼·彼得罗维奇·拜科夫斯基刚刚开完庭回来，正在书房里脱下手套，看着家庭女教师做报告，笑了

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当我开始规劝他时，他像往常一样用手指捂住耳朵，大声唱歌，淹没了我的声音。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 胆怯的, 怯懦的
vocabulary.context: The cowardly and faint-hearted did actually give up smoking, any who were somewhat more plucky and intelligent, after the thrashing took to carrying tobacco in the legs of their boots, and smoking in the barn.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 胆怯和胆怯的人实际上戒掉了吸烟，而那些稍微勇敢和聪明的人，在遭受殴打后开始在靴子里放烟草，并在谷仓里吸烟

vocabulary.definition: n. 悲凄
vocabulary.context: Again his eyes were clouded by mournfulness and something like fear; for a minute he looked pensively at the dark window, shuddered, and said, in a sinking voice:
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的眼睛再次被悲伤和类似恐惧的东西所蒙蔽。他若有所思地看着漆黑的窗户，浑身发抖，用低沉的声音说道：

vocabulary.definition: n. 冗长的废话, 繁琐费时的手续；adj. 冗长的, 无条理的
vocabulary.context: The prosecutor went on, thinking: "What next?" He spun out a long rigmarole, and ended like this:
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 检察官继续思考："接下来怎么办？"他滔滔不绝地说了一大堆废话，最后是这样的：

vocabulary.definition: n. 牙痛；[医] 牙痛
vocabulary.context: The pacing of the man overhead who, to judge from his nervous step, was thinking of something harassing, or was suffering from toothache, and the monotonous scales gave the stillness of the evening a drowsiness that disposed to lazy reveries.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 头顶上那个男人的踱步，从他紧张的步伐判断，他正在思考一些令人困扰的事情，或者正在遭受牙痛，单调的鳞片给夜晚的寂静带来了一种睡意，让人懒惰地沉思

vocabulary.definition: adj. 离题的, 东拉西扯的
vocabulary.context: And such light and discursive thoughts as visit the brain only when it is weary and resting began straying through Yevgeny Petrovitch's head; there is no telling whence and why they come, they do not remain long in the mind, but seem to glide over its surface without sinking deeply into it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 叶夫根尼·彼得罗维奇的头脑中开始出现诸如只有在疲倦和休息时才进入大脑的轻松而散漫的想法；不知道它们从何而来，为何而来，它们不会在脑海中停留太久，但似乎只是在其表面滑行，而没有深深地陷入其中

vocabulary.definition: n. 手摇风琴
vocabulary.context: Then he went on to describe how, while they were having dinner, a man with a hurdy-gurdy had come into the yard with a little girl, who had danced and sung to the music.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后他继续描述，当他们正在吃晚饭的时候，一个戴着手铐的男人带着一个小女孩走进院子，小女孩随着音乐跳舞唱歌

vocabulary.definition: adj. 面容苍白的, 前额有白斑的
vocabulary.context: He was a child whose sex could only have been guessed from his dress: weakly, white-faced, and fragile.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他是一个孩子，他的性别只能从他的衣着中猜出：虚弱、脸色苍白、脆弱

vocabulary.definition: n. 神圣不可侵犯性
vocabulary.context: "I am very anxious to reach your state," he wrote, "not just because of the great race now made for it but because of the sacredness of her soil washed by the blood of humanitarians for the cause of black freedom."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我非常渴望到达你们的州，"他写道，"不仅仅是因为现在有伟大的种族为此而生，而且因为她的土地被人道主义者为黑人自由事业的鲜血所洗净。"

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再卖, 转售；[经] 转卖, 再卖
vocabulary.context: These misleading advertisements were placed by land speculators who had bought up large amounts of land and hoped to resell it at a higher price.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些误导性广告是由土地投机者投放的，他们购买了大量土地并希望以更高的价格转售

vocabulary.definition: n. 投机者, 投机商人, 思索者；[经] 投机买卖者, 投机商
vocabulary.context: Beware of Speculators and Adventurers
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 谨防投机者和冒险家

vocabulary.definition: n. 小册子, 小册
vocabulary.context: Fancy advertisements and brochures and articles in newspapers enticed African Americans to move west.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 精美的广告、小册子和报纸上的文章吸引非裔美国人向西迁移

vocabulary.definition: n. 家园, 田产；[法] 宅基地, 住宅

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 宅地法

vocabulary.definition: v. 小心, 谨防
vocabulary.context: Beware of Speculators and Adventurers
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 谨防投机者和冒险家

vocabulary.definition: n. 大批的离去；[法] 退出, 大批离去, 成一外出
vocabulary.context: They began to think of it as a biblical "exodus" to the promised land, like the Bible story of Moses helping the slaves escape from Egypt to the Promised Land.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们开始将其视为圣经中"出埃及记"前往应许之地，就像圣经中摩西帮助奴隶逃离埃及前往应许之地的故事一样

vocabulary.definition: n. 察看, 洞察力, 见识；[医] 自知力, 洞察, 顿悟
vocabulary.context: The investigation that followed provided our earliest insight into the lived conditions of African Americans in the post-Civil War South, so even today, we owe a debt to the Exodusters who took that risk, leaving behind everything they knew to head west.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随后的调查为我们提供了对内战后南方非裔美国人生活状况的最早了解，因此即使在今天，我们仍欠那些冒着风险、抛下他们所知道的一切前往西部的逃亡者的恩情

vocabulary.definition: n. 无情；冷酷；残忍
vocabulary.context: To me, and I think to everyone who has seen it, that silent, persistent, haunting presence is the very embodiment of ruthlessness and untiring cruelty.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对我来说，我想对所有看过它的人来说，这种沉默、持久、令人难以忘怀的存在正是无情和不知疲倦的残忍的体现

vocabulary.definition: n. 无噪音；[网络] 无声；静音型空压机；闹中取境
vocabulary.context: It was terrible beyond expression, the utter noiselessness with which they resumed their places - apparently tireless; keeping pace with the racing horses without a sign of effort; patient as fate itself.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 可怕得无法形容，他们重新回到自己的位置时完全没有噪音--显然是不知疲倦；毫不费力地跟上赛马的步伐；耐心如同命运本身

vocabulary.definition: adj. 冰冻的；冷若冰霜的
vocabulary.context: ...leaving only ragged patches of white under overhanging boughs, and a thin coating of ice in the inequalities of the hard, frost-bound trail, making a treacherous footing for the horses' hoofs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...在悬垂的树枝下只留下了参差不齐的白色斑块，在坚硬、结霜的小道上留下了一层薄薄的冰，为马蹄提供了危险的立足点

vocabulary.definition: n. 美洲落叶松；[医] 北美落叶松
vocabulary.context: The first forty miles of the road had lain entirely over hills - zigzagging up one side of a mountain only to zigzag down the other - with the dense growth of pine and tamarack and cedar on both sides, wreathed here and there in mist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 前四十英里的路完全是在山上--蜿蜒地爬上山的一侧，然后又蜿蜒地走下另一侧--两边长满了茂密的松树、落叶松和雪松，到处都笼罩着薄雾

vocabulary.definition: n. 敬畏；v.t. 使敬畏
vocabulary.context: There, in the twilight and shadow, was the same silence, the same indistinctness, the same awing impression of motionless speed, the same horror of the inevitable, in that pursuit by the wolves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在暮色和阴影中，在狼群的追击中，同样的寂静，同样的模糊，同样令人敬畏的一动不动的速度，同样对不可避免的恐惧

vocabulary.definition: adj. 象鬼样的
vocabulary.context: But soon the stories of the old mountaineers came crowding into my mind, as the horses, terrified and snorting, plunged wildly along the narrow trail, while the ghostlike forms glided patiently alongside - appearing, disappearing, and reappearing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但很快，老登山者的故事就涌入了我的脑海，马匹受到惊吓，喷着鼻息，沿着狭窄的小路疯狂地冲去，而幽灵般的身影则耐心地在旁边滑行--出现、消失、再出现

vocabulary.definition: n. 脚声, 踏步, 脚步

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但连他们在雪地上的脚步声都听不见

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无声地, 静静地
vocabulary.context: There was not a sound - not a snap nor a snarl; but in the gathering twilight of the February evening, we saw them moving noiselessly over the thin coat of snow which covered the ground.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 没有任何声音--没有啪啪声，也没有咆哮声；没有任何声音。但在二月傍晚的暮色中，我们看到它们在覆盖地面的薄雪上无声无息地移动

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 烧成灰, 火葬
vocabulary.context: Workers then took the ashes of cremated victims from a common pile and placed them in urns to send to relatives, along with a certificate listing a fictive cause of death.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 工人们随后将火化遇难者的骨灰从公共堆中取出，放入骨灰盒中，连同列出虚构死因的证明一起寄给亲属

vocabulary.definition: n. 委婉说法, 委婉语
vocabulary.context: In the Nazi context, however, "euthanasia" was 3 4 a euphemism for a clandestine murder program of disabled patients living in institutional settings in Germany and German- annexed territories.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，在纳粹背景下，"安乐死"3 4 是一种委婉说法，指对生活在德国和德国吞并领土的机构环境中的残疾患者实施秘密谋杀计划

vocabulary.definition: adv. 在末端, 末尾, 每期
vocabulary.context: The term "euthanasia" (literally, "good death") usually refers to the inducement of a painless death for a chronically or terminally ill individual who would otherwise suffer.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "安乐死"（字面意思是"好死"）一词通常是指诱导慢性或绝症患者无痛死亡，否则他们将遭受痛苦

vocabulary.definition: adj. 老年病学的, 衰老的, 老年的；[医] 老年医学的, 老年病学的, 老人学的
vocabulary.context: The murder program continued until the last days of the war, expanding to include geriatric patients, bombing victims, and foreign forced unpleasant or offensive 4.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 谋杀计划一直持续到战争的最后几天，范围扩大到包括老年患者、爆炸受害者和外国强迫令人不快或冒犯的人4

vocabulary.definition: n. 配药量过多；v.t. 配药过量, 使过分沉溺
vocabulary.context: The clinics were in reality killing wards where specially recruited medical staff murdered their young charges by lethal overdoses or by starvation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些诊所实际上是杀戮病房，专门招募的医务人员通过致命的过量用药或饥饿来谋杀他们的年轻病人

vocabulary.definition: n. 职员, 负责人员, 官员；adj. 机能的, 作用的, 职务的

vocabulary.definition: adj. 隐蔽的, 偷偷摸摸的, 隐密的；n. 树丛, 隐藏处
vocabulary.context: The renewed effort relied closely upon local authorities and employed lethal injection and 11 12 starvation as more covert means of killing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 新的行动密切依赖地方当局，并采用注射死刑和11·12饥饿作为更隐蔽的杀戮手段

vocabulary.definition: n. 发射, 猎场, 射击
vocabulary.context: In the German-occupied East, SS and police units also murdered tens of thousands of disabled patients in mass shootings and gas vans.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在德占东部，党卫军和警察部队还通过大规模枪击和毒气车杀害了数万名残疾患者

vocabulary.definition: n. 聚会者, 召集人
vocabulary.context: On the last day of Passover, a white supremacist posted an antisemitic manifesto online before killing a congregant at Chabad Congregation in Poway, California.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 逾越节的最后一天，一名白人至上主义者在加利福尼亚州波威市查巴德会众杀害了一名会众之前，在网上发布了反犹太主义宣言

vocabulary.definition: n. 谣言, 误传
vocabulary.context: These hate crimes are often driven by antisemitic myths or canards.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些仇恨犯罪往往是由反犹太主义神话或谣言驱动的

vocabulary.definition: n. 杀神
vocabulary.context: Blood libel originated with the accusation that Jews murdered Jesus, known as deicide, and continues today with conspiracies about the powerful harming innocent children.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 血腥诽谤起源于对犹太人谋杀耶稣的指控，即所谓的"弑神者"，并在今天继续存在有关强权伤害无辜儿童的阴谋

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 变化, 产生变异, 变化元音；v.t. 使变异
vocabulary.context: While patterns repeat, antisemitism also constantly shifts form, mutating to persist in new eras.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在模式重复的同时，反犹太主义也在不断改变形式，变异以在新时代持续存在

vocabulary.definition: n. 秘密结社, (尤指政治上的)阴谋；v.i. 策划阴谋
vocabulary.context: The first was The Protocols of the Elders of Zion , a conspiracy hoax that was published in Russia, in 1903, which claimed a hidden Jewish cabal of "Zionists" sought to control the global economy and media from the shadows.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第一个是《锡安长老议定书》，这是 1903 年在俄罗斯出版的阴谋骗局，声称一个隐藏的犹太"犹太复国主义者"阴谋集团试图从暗处控制全球经济和媒体

vocabulary.definition: n. 攻击者；[法] 攻击者, 行凶者
vocabulary.context: In December, two gunmen killed four people at a Kosher supermarket in Jersey City, New Jersey; and during Chanukah, a lone assailant stabbed five celebrants at a Rabbi's New York home, killing one.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 12月，两名枪手在新泽西州泽西市的一家Kosher超市杀害了四人；光明节期间，一名袭击者在纽约拉比的家中刺伤了五名庆祝者，造成一人死亡

vocabulary.definition: adj. 反犹太人的；[网络] 应用数学和反犹太观；反犹的；以反闪的
vocabulary.context: In the investigation that followed the massacre, analysts found a slew of antisemitic posts on the shooter's social media accounts.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在大屠杀后的调查中，分析人士在枪手的社交媒体账户上发现了大量反犹太主义帖子

vocabulary.definition: adj. 历史悠久的
vocabulary.context: This misconception allows antisemitism to persist in centuries-old cycles.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种误解使得反犹太主义能够持续几个世纪之久

vocabulary.definition: n. 不忠实, 不忠行为, 不义
vocabulary.context: Disloyalty: Jews have nearly always lived among other nations as a minority.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 不忠诚：犹太人几乎一直作为少数群体生活在其他民族中

vocabulary.definition: v. (使)交织, 织进, (使)混杂
vocabulary.context: "That was the biggest challenge," Belzberg says: "to interweave in a coherent and lyrical way that people can be taken from one story to the next and not be fatigued, but be enriched by it."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这是最大的挑战，"贝尔兹伯格说："以一种连贯和抒情的方式交织在一起，让人们从一个故事到下一个故事，而不是感到疲劳，而是从中得到丰富。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 优秀策划者, 才子；v.t. 指导, 主持, 策划
vocabulary.context: Tehlirian later killed one of the masterminds of the massacre, Talaat Pasha, who was living freely.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特利里安后来杀死了大屠杀的主谋之一塔拉特帕夏，而他却过着自由自在的生活

vocabulary.definition: adj. 抒情诗调的, 有抒情味的, 感情丰富的
vocabulary.context: "That was the biggest challenge," Belzberg says: "to interweave in a coherent and lyrical way that people can be taken from one story to the next and not be fatigued, but be enriched by it."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这是最大的挑战，"贝尔兹伯格说："以一种连贯和抒情的方式交织在一起，让人们从一个故事到下一个故事，而不是感到疲劳，而是从中得到丰富。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 语言学家
vocabulary.context: Raphael Lemkin is the Polish lawyer and linguist who coined the term "genocide" - and dedicated his life to making genocide recognized as a crime.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 拉斐尔·莱姆金 (Raphael Lemkin) 是一位波兰律师和语言学家，他创造了"种族灭绝"一词，并致力于将种族灭绝定为犯罪

vocabulary.definition: adj. 互相耦合的, 清晰的, 明了的，凝聚性的, 连贯的
vocabulary.context: "That was the biggest challenge," Belzberg says: "to interweave in a coherent and lyrical way that people can be taken from one story to the next and not be fatigued, but be enriched by it."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这是最大的挑战，"贝尔兹伯格说："以一种连贯和抒情的方式交织在一起，让人们从一个故事到下一个故事，而不是感到疲劳，而是从中得到丰富。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 蒸馏室, 寂静, 剧照；v. 蒸馏, (使)平静, (使)静止；adj. 静止的, 不动的, 静寂的, 不起泡的, 静物摄影的；adv. 仍然, 更, 静止地；conj. 然而, 但是
vocabulary.context: Belzberg worked from 800 hours of footage and 5,000 stills to tell a story that spans a century.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 贝尔茨伯格利用 800 小时的镜头和 5,000 张剧照讲述了一个跨越一个世纪的故事

vocabulary.definition: adv. 坚持地; 固执地; 一个劲儿
vocabulary.context: Once he'd established the word, Lemkin worked persistently in the then-newly-formed United Nations, hounding delegates to discuss his new word and acknowledge the issue.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一旦确立了这个词，莱姆金就在当时新成立的联合国中坚持不懈地工作，吸引代表们讨论他的新词并承认这个问题

vocabulary.definition: adj. 负有责任的, 可说明的, 可解释的；[经] 负有责任的
vocabulary.context: That's what really enraged Lemkin: He felt that a leader shouldn't have to cross a border in order to be held accountable for their crimes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这才是真正激怒莱姆金的原因：他认为领导人不应该为了对自己的罪行负责而跨越国界

vocabulary.definition: n. 发行, 问题, 后果, 流出, 出口, 争端；v.i. 发行, 流出, 造成...结果, 传下；v.t. 使流出, 放出, 发行, 发布, 发给
vocabulary.context: Once he'd established the word, Lemkin worked persistently in the then-newly-formed United Nations, hounding delegates to discuss his new word and acknowledge the issue.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一旦确立了这个词，莱姆金就在当时新成立的联合国中坚持不懈地工作，吸引代表们讨论他的新词并承认这个问题

vocabulary.definition: adv. 自治地；独立自主地
vocabulary.context: Drones may fly autonomously (along a preprogrammed path) or under the control of a pilot on the ground.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 无人机可以自主飞行（沿着预先编程的路径）或在地面飞行员的控制下飞行

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未被发现的
vocabulary.context: High in the sky, and under the cover of darkness, the Terrapin 1 flew undetected over the landscape.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在夜色的掩护下，Terrapin 1 号在高空飞行，未被发现

vocabulary.definition: v. 调整, 使有规则( fine-tune的现在分词 )
vocabulary.context: Farmers could then respond by fine-tuning the way they care for the plants in that corner of their fields.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后，农民可以通过微调他们照顾田地角落植物的方式来做出回应

vocabulary.definition: n. 软式小型飞船, 顽固的保守分子
vocabulary.context: Some fly like an airplane, others like a helicopter or a blimp.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有些像飞机一样飞行，有些像直升机或飞艇一样飞行

vocabulary.definition: n. 轮廓；v.t. 画轮廓；adj. 显示轮廓的；[计] 轮廓
vocabulary.context: These light detection and ranging, or LIDAR, systems can map the shape and contour of the landscape by firing laser pulses at the ground.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些光探测和测距（LIDAR）系统可以通过向地面发射激光脉冲来绘制地形的形状和轮廓

vocabulary.definition: n. 水龟
vocabulary.context: High in the sky, and under the cover of darkness, the Terrapin 1 flew undetected over the landscape.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在夜色的掩护下，Terrapin 1 号在高空飞行，未被发现

vocabulary.definition: n. 天鹅绒领子暗钮长大衣, 坐卧两用长沙发
vocabulary.context: This engineer's company, Volt Aerial Robotics, builds drones in Chesterfield, Mo.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这位工程师的公司 Volt Aerial Robotics 在密苏里州切斯特菲尔德制造无人机

vocabulary.definition: adv. 潇洒地, 火辣辣地, 刺痛地
vocabulary.context: And that means using drones smartly and efficiently.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这意味着智能、高效地使用无人机

vocabulary.definition: n. 富有冒险性的事, 危险
vocabulary.context: And in her experiments, volunteers' estimates of storms' riskiness systematically declined as names became more feminine, suggesting that a subtle form of sexism influences people's willingness to prepare for hurricanes, she proposes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她提出，在她的实验中，随着名字变得更加女性化，志愿者对风暴风险的估计系统性下降，这表明一种微妙的性别歧视会影响人们为飓风做准备的意愿

vocabulary.definition: adv. 带有成见地(stereotype的副词形式)
vocabulary.context: "Giving human names to hurricanes may bring to mind qualities that are stereotypically associated with women or men, like mildness or aggressiveness, causing perceived qualities of the hurricane to shift toward those traits," Shavitt says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "给飓风起人名可能会让人想起人们对女性或男性的刻板印象，比如温和或攻击性，从而导致飓风的感知品质转向这些特征，"沙维特说

vocabulary.definition: n. 温和, 温暖
vocabulary.context: "Giving human names to hurricanes may bring to mind qualities that are stereotypically associated with women or men, like mildness or aggressiveness, causing perceived qualities of the hurricane to shift toward those traits," Shavitt says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "给飓风起人名可能会让人想起人们对女性或男性的刻板印象，比如温和或攻击性，从而导致飓风的感知品质转向这些特征，"沙维特说

vocabulary.definition: v. 站起身, 提高（价格等）( up的现在分词 ); 突然移动, 突然（做意想不到的事）
vocabulary.context: As a result, those in the path of an oncoming "female" hurricane are less likely to evacuate or take other precautions, upping the storm's death toll, the researchers conclude June 2 in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究人员在 6 月 2 日《美国国家科学院院刊》上得出结论，因此，那些处于即将到来的"女性"飓风路径上的人不太可能撤离或采取其他预防措施，从而增加了风暴的死亡人数

vocabulary.definition: adj. 实在的, 现实的
vocabulary.context: Whether people actually take fewer precautions against real-life hurricanes with feminine names than storms with male names is still an open question, says psychologist David Funder of the University of California, Riverside.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 加州大学河滨分校的心理学家大卫·芬德表示，现实生活中人们对女性名字的飓风采取的预防措施是否比男性名字的风暴更少，这仍然是一个悬而未决的问题

vocabulary.definition: adj. 统计的, 统计上的, 统计学的；[经] 统计的, 统计学的
vocabulary.context: Other factors, such as whether storms were strong and fast-moving or moderate and slow-moving, could have affected preparedness and created a false statistical impression that gender expectations played a role, he says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他说，其他因素，例如风暴是强而快速移动还是中度且缓慢移动，可能会影响准备工作，并造成性别期望发挥作用的错误统计印象

vocabulary.definition: n. 阳性, 男性；adj. 男性的, 阳性的, 有丈夫气的
vocabulary.context: A severe hurricane with a masculine name caused an average of about 15 deaths, compared with an average of around 42 deaths for a severe hurricane with a feminine name, the researchers estimate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究人员估计，具有男性名称的严重飓风平均造成约 15 人死亡，而具有女性名称的严重飓风平均造成约 42 人死亡

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 祈求, 恳求, 实行, 援引, 引起；[计] 调用; 请求
vocabulary.context: "A hurricane bearing down on your city surely invokes different psychological processes than reading an online scenario about a hurricane."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "飓风袭击你的城市肯定会引发与阅读有关飓风的在线场景不同的心理过程。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 枪战
vocabulary.context: The infamous gunfight at the OK Corral in Tombstone, a town in the Arizona Territory, did take place.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 亚利桑那领地墓碑镇 OK Corral 确实发生了臭名昭著的枪战

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 把...编成小说, 使小说化
vocabulary.context: This fictionalized version of the West was repeated on the big screen once movies became popular.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 电影流行后，这个虚构的西方世界就在大银幕上重演

vocabulary.definition: n. 治安官, 警官, 总管；[法] 警察, 警官, 巡警
vocabulary.context: In a place full of outlaws (literally those who lived "outside the law") and no government law officers, local constables, sheriffs, and marshals were left to enforce order.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在一个充满不法之徒（字面意思是那些生活在"法律之外"的人）的地方，没有政府执法人员、当地警察、治安官和法警负责执行秩序

vocabulary.definition: n. [俚]充当男性角色的女同性恋者；粗鲁的男人或男孩
vocabulary.context: The Wild Bunch, a gang formed by Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid, was real.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由布奇·卡西迪和圣丹斯小子组成的"狂野帮"是真实存在的

vocabulary.definition: n. 放逐, 驱逐；[法] 驱逐出境, 放逐, 流利
vocabulary.context: Everyone in the group had to follow them or risk banishment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 小组中的每个人都必须跟随他们，否则就有被驱逐的危险

vocabulary.definition: n. 州的状态(或地位)；[法] 国家地位, 州的地位
vocabulary.context: The laws and justice systems that came along with statehood did not apply.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 建国时附带的法律和司法系统并不适用

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 超过, 逃脱
vocabulary.context: Others went west to outrun the law.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其他人则前往西部以逃避法律

vocabulary.definition: n. 元帅, 陆空军高级将官, 典礼官, 执法官；v.t. 整理, 引领, 统率；v.i. 排列, 各就各位
vocabulary.context: In a place full of outlaws (literally those who lived "outside the law") and no government law officers, local constables, sheriffs, and marshals were left to enforce order.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在一个充满不法之徒（字面意思是那些生活在"法律之外"的人）的地方，没有政府执法人员、当地警察、治安官和法警负责执行秩序

vocabulary.definition: n. 圣洁；慈爱；至善
vocabulary.context: I was a self-righteous child, a great moralist, a firm believer in personal spiritual struggle and my own saintliness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我是一个自以为是的孩子，一个伟大的道德家，一个个人精神斗争和我自己的圣洁的坚定信徒

vocabulary.definition: n. 健谈者
vocabulary.context: After church, I am the last schmoozer on the sidewalk.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 教堂结束后，我是人行道上最后一个闲聊的人

vocabulary.definition: adj. 脾气随和的, 逍遥自在的, 悠闲的, 懒散的
vocabulary.context: My father was an easygoing man, but I'd heard him gear up on the subject of integration and I thought he might give a good twist to this fellow's ear.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我的父亲是一个随和的人，但我听到他在整合这个话题上做好了准备，我想他可能会给这个家伙带来好消息

vocabulary.definition: n. 高兴, 酒宴
vocabulary.context: I was pretty depressed by all this jollity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所有这些欢乐让我感到非常沮丧

vocabulary.definition: n. (华丽的)装饰, (华丽的)服饰, 鲜艳服装, 幽雅, 精炼炉；[化] 木炭吹炼炉; 木炭精炼炉
vocabulary.context: After mass, I was required to stand in front of church admiring all those pastel dresses and bone-colored pumps, enduring the giddy talk of folks in their new finery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 弥撒结束后，我被要求站在教堂前，欣赏那些柔和的裙子和骨色的高跟鞋，忍受人们穿着新服饰的令人眼花缭乱的谈话

vocabulary.definition: n. 美好, 准确, 精密, 拘泥细节, 纤细
vocabulary.context: I was so aggrieved at all the social niceties involved in dressing for Easter - those were the days when we still wore hats to church - that I hardly registered a whole town awash in gauzy light.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我对复活节着装所涉及的所有社交礼仪感到非常愤慨--那些日子里我们仍然戴着帽子去教堂--以至于我几乎没有注意到整个小镇都沐浴在薄纱般的灯光下

vocabulary.definition: adj. 粗俗的, 愚钝的, 粗糙的, 非常的
vocabulary.context: The whole fluttery Easter parade - so indulgent, so crass, so commercial - offended my sense of dignity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 整个复活节游行--如此放纵、如此粗鲁、如此商业化--冒犯了我的尊严感

vocabulary.definition: adv. 巧合地；一致地
vocabulary.context: As it happened, he smiled a distant, pleasant smile (he was, perhaps not coincidentally, a psychologist), and said: "Oh, I couldn't pull the children out now.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 结果，他露出了一个遥远而愉快的微笑（也许并非巧合，他是一位心理学家），并说道："哦，我现在无法把孩子们拉出来

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 教化, 使有德行；v.i. 说教
vocabulary.context: (And what a picture they must have had of me: scowling, tapping my foot, skulking away if anyone got too close.) One of those Easter Sundays - bright, airy, perfumed - I ended up paired on the sidewalk with my father, who had always poked good-natured fun at my moralizing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: （他们对我的印象一定是：皱着眉头，跺着脚，如果有人靠得太近，就躲开。）有一个复活节周日--明亮、通风、芳香四溢--最后我和父亲在人行道上结成了一对，他总是善意地取笑我的说教

vocabulary.definition: adj. 焦急不安的
vocabulary.context: The whole fluttery Easter parade - so indulgent, so crass, so commercial - offended my sense of dignity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 整个复活节游行--如此放纵、如此粗鲁、如此商业化--冒犯了我的尊严感

vocabulary.definition: n. 射击名手, 神枪手, 奸商
vocabulary.context: Women were everything from schoolteachers to sharpshooters, homemakers to hotel keepers, and their contributions during this period of time left a mark.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从学校教师到神枪手，从家庭主妇到酒店老板，女性无所不包，她们在这段时期的贡献留下了印记

vocabulary.definition: n. 边疆居民
vocabulary.context: Due to a high demand for entertainment among male frontiersmen, many women made a living as musicians, dancers, and actresses.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于男性拓荒者对娱乐的高需求，许多女性以音乐家、舞蹈家和女演员为生

vocabulary.definition: n. 剁碎的肉, 肉馅, 百果馅
vocabulary.context: Marshbanks once made a mincemeat pie so divine that the man who ate it later bought her a silk dress the moment he struck gold.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 马什班克斯曾经制作过一个非常美味的肉馅饼，以至于吃了它的那个人后来在他挖到金子的那一刻就给她买了一件丝绸连衣裙

vocabulary.definition: n. 射手, 狙击兵, 神射手；[法] 按手印者, 不能用笔署名者
vocabulary.context: An extraordinarily skilled marksman, Annie Oakley became one of the headline performers for Buffalo Bill's Wild West Show.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 安妮·奥克利 (Annie Oakley) 是一位技艺精湛的射手，成为布法罗比尔狂野西部秀 (Buffalo Bill's Wild West Show) 的主角之一

vocabulary.definition: n. 勘探者, 采矿者
vocabulary.context: By the 1860s, women made up only 19 percent of prospectors; that didn't stop women like Lillian Malcolm, who left the Broadway stage to become a gold prospector in Alaska.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 到 1860 年代，女性仅占探矿者的 19%；但这并没有阻止像莉莲·马尔科姆这样的女性，她离开了百老汇舞台，成为阿拉斯加的一名淘金者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未经驯服的, 不能抑制的, 难控驭的
vocabulary.context: The West, on the other hand, was a new and untamed place.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 另一方面，西部是一个新的、未开化的地方

vocabulary.definition: n. 大厅, 沙龙, 酒吧, 会客室
vocabulary.context: Some even owned their own saloons and music halls.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有些人甚至拥有自己的沙龙和音乐厅

vocabulary.definition: n. 统治, 统辖, 管理；[法] 统治, 管理, 支配
vocabulary.context: But a more lax society and less governance meant women in the West also seized opportunities to make a living for themselves outside of their domestic skills.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但更加宽松的社会和较少的治理意味着西方妇女也抓住机会在家庭技能之外为自己谋生

vocabulary.definition: n. 防风林, 防风物

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 自耕农在小溪或小河附近寻找土地，那里有连绵起伏的山丘，可以作为防风林

vocabulary.definition: adj. 耐火的, 防火的；v.i. 变成有耐火性
vocabulary.context: Ultimately, these homes, which earned settlers the nickname "sodbusters," were cool in the summer, warm in the winter, cozy year-round, and nearly fireproof.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最终，这些为定居者赢得了"除草者"绰号的房屋，夏天凉爽，冬天温暖，全年舒适，而且几乎防火

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1850 年，拉科塔苏族部落发现，有 50 万人（其中许多人在加利福尼亚州寻找黄金）穿越了他们的土地中心，耗尽了他们的资源，扰乱了他们的生活方式
